rate cut save the crypto Market from
mispriced fairy tales about liquidity

explicitly calling for the FED to begin
cutting the policy rate in July as
evidenced by the crisis of confidence
being signaled by global financial
markets we do not believe the FED will
op for an intermediate cut based on
current conditions we do believe they
will if the repo Andor credit Market
sees up in the coming weeks those
dyamics are impossible to predict so it
will be important for investors to
continue relying on the risk management
overlays featured in kiss or in the
proper trade recommendations prescribed
by our discretionary riskmanagement
overlay AKA Dr Mo to successfully

recall that both systems began raising

early June and reducing 42 macro client

crypto in late June while continuing to

began unwinding last week trust the
process and the process is signaling

and late junee as well and treasury

to short Max position from short half
position on on June 27th sorry on July
27th rather Commodities and base medals
were each cut to no position from long
half position on on July 27th uh the

from short half position on July 27th

from no position on July 27th before the
big down move that we saw in the US

highlights here there's obviously other

market and fixed income Market that Dr

to on the risk asset side of things and
increased exposure to on the defensive
asset side of things so as I mentioned
very proud of it our systems work you

always from our 42 macro uh Community
here this one's regarding gold this is
uh Gold's at the beginning of
transitions so it reads High areas based
on your extensive experience I like to
hear your thoughts on this uh I expect
the go prices to rise strongly due to
geopolitical pressures and inflation
fears but has remained within a range
I've read some articles suggesting that
there's heavy selling and risk assets
gold can be negatively affected despite
being considered a safe haven would you
believe SP your perspective on this
thanks for your valuable time so yes
this great question by the client that's
exactly what we have observed as well is
that in times of actual severe Market
stress you know thinking about 2008 2011
2020 uh we have seen
investors reach for things like gold and
other assets that have historically done
well in deflationary types of
environments uh and use that as a source
of funds uh uh to take down overall
portfolio risk um you we actually saw
that in the financial crisis where you
saw investment grade credit spreads
actually Gap wider than high yield
credit spreads because investors were
unable to sell their high yield bonds
and so they in order to take down
broader portfolio risk and raise cash
they were selling what they could sell
as opposed to what they should have sold
and so uh we see you know we've seen
this in uh in Gold specifically as most
recently is March of 2020 we saw it in
the treasury Barn Market as well in
March of 2020 when investors were
liquidating treasuries because they
couldn't get out of their equity and
crypto exposures and and credit
exposures fast enough so just be aware
of that if you certainly can see these
things occur you know it's hard to
predict it's hard to forecast these
types of Market developments but what we
do know is that whenever the positioning
cycle gets very one-sided like we've
been warning about uh with respect to
the medium to long-term positioning
indicators featured in our positioning
model signaling elevated risk of a of a
crash in Risk assets for the past couple
of months we have been flagging that to
for clients when the positioning cycle
uh gets as as as as one-sided as it
becomes then you start to see these
kinds of squirly aggressive uh uh
high-speed market unwinds and and and
and and you know kind of cross asset
repricing Dynamics take place I.E gold
being used as a source of funds for
people who can't get out of equity and
credit position so again it's hard to
predict these types of uh systems but
this is why we rely on our quantitative
Risk Management Systems when it comes to
you know building or or taking down
positions in our portfolio as opposed to
what the client is talking about which
is I thought this therefore I thought
this therefore that's not the right way
to manage risk if you learn if you
people learn anything from me before I
get hit by a bus is you do not manage
risk on the basis of your predictions
you manage risk on the basis of your
quantitative risk management signals if
they tell you to take up exposure to an
asset class you take up exposure and
ignore what you thought if they tell you
to take down exposure to an asset class
you take down exposure and ignore what
you previously thought what you think
about the future should inform you on
what types of Securities you want to buy
and sell but they should not be
informing your decision to buy and sell
those securities leave that stuff to the
quantitative risk management signals and
if you don't have you know the very good
quantitative risk management signals to
rely on we obviously make those
available to investors all around the
world at a very affordable price point
and that is my promise to you guys until
I'm retired so we'll wrap it up there
